name: Tidewater Ombre
shortDescription: A portrait sea-glass mirror framed in white wood, its border washed in a turquoise-to-cobalt ombre of crushed glass with a natural shell and starfish cluster gathered at the shore.
description: A handcrafted mirror that captures the exact moment the shallows deepen into open water. The border is built by hand from genuine sea glass and crushed glass, graduated from pale turquoise at the top through teal and sapphire to a deep cobalt below, each fragment set in clear resin so it catches light like wet stone. Along the lower edge, a collection of natural Gulf shells, scallops and two dried starfish gather as though carried in by the tide, softening the geometry of the white-painted wood frame. The generous beveled mirror at the center reflects the room while the glasswork frames it in color. Individually made in Englewood, Florida, and entirely one-of-a-kind, no two borders ever fall the same way.
story: Mary builds these borders the way the Gulf builds a shoreline, in layers. She begins at the top with the palest turquoise and works downward into deeper blues, hand-placing each shard of sea glass before flooding it with resin so the color reads like water finding its depth. The shells and starfish gathered at the base are real, collected and cured, arranged by hand so the piece feels less constructed than washed ashore.
care: Dust gently with a dry, soft cloth. Keep out of prolonged direct sun to preserve the sea-glass tones, and hang from a secure wall anchor. Avoid liquid cleaners on the glass, shell and resin surfaces.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.
seoTitle: Tidewater Ombre Sea Glass Mirror | Handmade Coastal Wall Mirror
seoDescription: Handcrafted sea-glass mirror with a turquoise-to-cobalt ombre crushed-glass border, natural shells and starfish in a white wood frame. One-of-a-kind coastal decor from Englewood, FL.
altText: Portrait white-framed mirror with a turquoise-to-cobalt crushed sea-glass border and a cluster of natural shells and starfish along the lower edge.

name: Tidewater Reflection
shortDescription: An arched mirror framed in a shoreline of crushed aqua sea glass, natural starfish and scattered shells, set in weathered whitewashed wood.
description: A handmade statement mirror that brings the quiet glamour of the Gulf shoreline indoors. The softly arched looking glass is surrounded by a thick, glistening border of crushed sea glass in shades of seafoam, aqua and clear surf-tumbled tones, set in resin so it catches the light like wet sand at low tide. Genuine dried starfish anchor opposite corners, while natural cockle and scallop shells and a few luminous pearls are tucked along the edges, as though the tide left them behind. The whole piece is cradled in a reclaimed wood frame, whitewashed and gently distressed to echo sun-bleached dock wood. Each shell is placed by hand and no two mirrors are ever alike.
story: Mary Lee gathers her glass and shells along the Englewood beaches of Florida's Gulf Coast, sorting each piece by color and tide-worn softness before building her borders one shard at a time. This mirror began with a length of weathered dock wood and a handful of seafoam glass, layered and set in resin over several days so the shoreline seems to ripple right up to the edge of the glass. The starfish were chosen for their symmetry, the pearls for the way they catch morning light. It is meant to hang where it can hold a little of the sky and the sea at once.
care: Dust gently with a dry, soft cloth. Keep out of prolonged direct sun to preserve the sea-glass tones, and hang from a secure wall anchor. Avoid liquid cleaners on the glass, shell and resin surfaces.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.
seoTitle: Tidewater Reflection Sea Glass Mirror | Handmade Coastal Wood-Framed Mirror
seoDescription: Handcrafted sea glass mirror with crushed aqua glass, natural starfish and shells in a whitewashed wood frame. A one-of-a-kind coastal statement piece by Sea Attitudes by Mary Lee.
altText: Handmade arched mirror bordered with crushed aqua sea glass, natural starfish, shells and pearls in a weathered whitewashed wood frame.

name: Turquoise Tide Mirror
shortDescription: A wood-framed mirror edged in crushed turquoise and clear sea glass, crowned with a natural starfish and a shoreline of shells.
description: A handcrafted wall mirror framed in weathered gray wood, its glass surface bordered by a glittering mosaic of crushed sea glass that fades from deep turquoise into a frosted clear shore. A natural sugar starfish anchors the upper corner, while a scatter of luminous faux pearls and a low tideline of real seashells and crushed black glass settle along the base like sand at the water's edge. Each piece of glass is hand-laid and set in clear resin, so no two mirrors are ever alike. The reflective center keeps the composition light and airy, making it equally at home above a coastal console, in a powder room, or leaning on a bedroom dresser.
story: Mary Lee builds each mirror the way the Gulf builds a beach: layer by patient layer. The turquoise here was sorted by hand from genuine and crushed glass to capture that exact shade of shallow Englewood water on a clear morning, then graded into clear toward the mirror so the eye reads it as surf meeting sand. The starfish and shells were gathered along Florida's Gulf Coast, and the dark crushed-glass shoreline grounds the whole piece in the wrack line where the prettiest finds always wash up. It is a one-of-a-kind reflection of the sea she lives beside.
care: Dust gently with a dry, soft cloth. Keep out of prolonged direct sun to preserve the sea-glass tones, and hang from a secure wall anchor. Avoid liquid cleaners on the glass, shell and resin surfaces.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.
seoTitle: Turquoise Tide Sea Glass Mirror | Handmade Coastal Wall Mirror
seoDescription: Handcrafted sea-glass wall mirror bordered in crushed turquoise glass with a natural starfish, shells and pearls in a weathered wood frame. One-of-a-kind coastal decor from Englewood, FL.
altText: Portrait wall mirror in a weathered gray wood frame, bordered with crushed turquoise and clear sea glass, a natural starfish in the upper left, pearls, and a row of seashells along the bottom.

name: Tidewater Starfish
shortDescription: A whitewashed wood mirror edged in glittering sea-glass surf, with a natural shell bed and a single starfish resting at the crest.
description: A portrait mirror framed in soft, whitewashed weathered wood and bordered entirely in crushed sea glass that catches the light like sunlit shoreline foam. The pale aqua and frosted-clear glass gathers toward the base into a true tide line, where a bed of natural seashells - scallops, cockles and tiny spirals - settles into dark sand-toned grains. A creamy starfish clings to the upper right corner, the quiet focal point of the piece. Each shell is hand-placed and the glass hand-laid in resin, so no two mirrors are ever the same. The result is calm, luminous and unmistakably coastal - a reflection framed by the sea itself.
story: Mary builds each of these mirrors the way the Gulf builds a shoreline - one layer at a time. She lays the crushed sea glass by hand around the reflective center, letting it crest and break toward a lower shell bed gathered from genuine Florida beach finds. The lone starfish in the corner is chosen last, set where the eye naturally rests. Made one piece at a time in her Englewood studio, this mirror carries the calm of a low-tide morning into any room.
care: Dust gently with a dry, soft cloth. Keep out of prolonged direct sun to preserve the sea-glass tones, and hang from a secure wall anchor. Avoid liquid cleaners on the glass, shell and resin surfaces.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.

name: Sea Foam Shoreline
shortDescription: A crushed sea-glass mirror edged in pale aqua glass, with a collected shoreline of natural shells and a single starfish resting at the surface.
description: A handmade mirror that frames your reflection in the colors of the shallows. Pale aqua sea glass is crushed and set by hand around the glass, catching the light like sun on a tide pool, while the lower edge gives way to a gathered shoreline of natural shells - scallops, cockles and clams in soft cream, sand and faint rose. A lone white starfish drifts at the upper corner, the quiet finishing note. Bordered in a clean white wood frame, it brings the calm of the Gulf Coast to an entry, a powder room or a vanity. Individually handcrafted in Englewood, Florida; no two are ever alike.
story: Made along Florida's Gulf Coast, this piece begins with glass tumbled and crushed by hand, then layered around the mirror until the border glints like water over sand. The shells were gathered from local beaches and arranged one by one into a shoreline at the base, each chosen for its shape and shade. Set in resin and finished in a bright white frame, it is a one-of-a-kind keepsake of the coast.
care: Dust gently with a dry, soft cloth. Keep out of prolonged direct sun to preserve the sea-glass tones, and hang from a secure wall anchor. Avoid liquid cleaners on the glass, shell and resin surfaces.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.

name: Gilded Tide
shortDescription: A crest of aqua sea glass rises over a gold-leafed shoreline, flanked by sea stars and scallop shells in a weathered whitewashed shadow box.
description: A swell of genuine aqua sea glass crests at the center of this shadow box, its tumbled fragments catching the light like sunlit shallows. Along its base runs a hand-applied ribbon of gold leaf, suggesting the gilded line where surf meets sand. Two soft-blue sea stars and a pair of natural scallop shells rest below on a painted shoreline, grounding the composition in true coastal calm. The piece is set in a distressed whitewashed wood frame, its worn edges revealing warm wood and flecks of gold beneath. Individually handmade and entirely one-of-a-kind.
story: Made on Florida's Gulf Coast in Englewood, this piece began with sea glass tumbled soft by the tide and shells gathered along the shore. Mary Lee layers each fragment by hand, finishing the meeting of glass and sand with delicate gold leaf so the work shimmers the way the beach does at low light. No two are ever alike.
care: Dust gently with a dry, soft cloth and keep out of prolonged direct sun. Display indoors, away from moisture; avoid liquid cleaners on the glass and shell surfaces.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.

name: Beach Please Mermaid
shortDescription: A serene sea-green mermaid drifts through a swirled resin tide, framed in weathered whitewashed wood with a playful "Beach Please" finish.
description: A reclining mermaid in soft sea-green floats across a hand-poured resin sea, its swirls and bubbles capturing the slow drift of a Gulf Coast tide. Genuine sea glass and crushed glass settle along the lower shore, where real seashells, a sun-bleached coral fragment and two slim teal starfish gather as if washed in on the last wave. A hammered sea-green starfish anchors the upper corner, and a pair of hand-painted wooden fish spell out "Beach Please" along the base. The frame is distressed whitewashed wood, its worn brown undertones softened by salt and time, giving the piece the quiet patina of something found rather than made.
story: Made by hand in Englewood, Florida, this shadow box gathers the colors of the Gulf at dusk, when the water turns to muted glass and the shoreline keeps the day's small treasures. Each shell, starfish and shard of sea glass is placed individually, so no two mermaids ever rest in quite the same tide. The "Beach Please" lettering carries the unhurried wit of barefoot afternoons, a gentle reminder to slow down and let the sea set the pace.
care: Dust gently with a dry, soft cloth and keep out of prolonged direct sun. Display indoors, away from moisture; avoid liquid cleaners on the glass and shell surfaces.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.

name: Loggerhead Tide
shortDescription: A young sea turtle glides across layered cobalt and aqua sea glass toward a sun-warmed shore of shells and copper sand.
description: A single sea turtle paddles through a glittering current of crushed sea glass, its hand-painted amber shell catching the light against deep navy and bright turquoise water. A warm gold sun rises in the upper corner, while the shoreline below is built from genuine scallop and spiral shells nestled into glints of copper-brown sand. Everything is set in glass-smooth resin and bordered by a softly distressed whitewashed wood frame, giving the scene the cool, weathered feel of sun-bleached wood. Compact enough for a shelf, a gallery wall, or a windowsill where the light can move through the glass.
story: Mary builds each underwater scene by hand, sorting sea glass by shade to pour the gradient from the darkest depths up into the shallows. The turtle is placed last, mid-stroke, as though it simply wandered into the frame. No two of these tides ever set the same way.
care: Dust gently with a dry, soft cloth and keep out of prolonged direct sun. Display indoors, away from moisture; avoid liquid cleaners on the glass and shell surfaces.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.

name: Garnet Octopus
shortDescription: A garnet-red sea-glass octopus drifts through misty teal water in this handmade coastal shadow box, framed in weathered weathered gray wood.
description: A deep garnet octopus, hand-built from thousands of fragments of crushed sea glass, hovers over a luminous seabed in this one-of-a-kind shadow box. Above it, clouds of aqua and seafoam glass bloom like soft coral against a rippling, water-washed background, while a cobalt and sky-blue glass floor glitters at the base. Real Gulf Coast seashells and a single dried starfish nestle into the sand line, and the octopus regards you with a pair of playful, gleaming eyes. Set in a weathered gray-wood frame and sealed under glass, the piece holds genuine depth and movement, the colors shifting with the light throughout the day. Individually handmade in Englewood, Florida, no two are ever alike.
story: Inspired by the curious octopuses that tuck into the rocks along Florida's Gulf shore, this scene began with a handful of deep red glass and the idea of a creature both regal and a little mischievous. Each fragment was placed by hand, layer over layer, until the octopus seemed to swim.
care: Dust gently with a dry, soft cloth and keep out of prolonged direct sun. Display indoors, away from moisture; avoid liquid cleaners on the glass and shell surfaces.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.

name: Seafoam Loggerhead
shortDescription: A seafoam sea-glass loggerhead gliding above a ribbon of aqua surf, finished with a single pearl eye.
description: A solitary sea turtle drifts across this shadow box, his shell and flippers built entirely from soft seafoam-green sea glass laid piece by piece until the whole form catches the light. Beneath him, a current of aqua and cerulean crushed glass rolls like the Gulf at low tide, grounding the scene in motion. A single freshwater pearl marks his eye, the one quiet point of stillness in the swim. Set deep within a clean white shadow-box frame, the piece reads as both a coastal keepsake and a small meditation on the creatures that have crossed these waters for millions of years. Individually handmade on Florida's Gulf Coast, no two turtles are ever alike.
story: Loggerheads nest along the beaches of Englewood each summer, and this piece began as a tribute to those slow, ancient travelers. Each shard of green glass was hand-placed to follow the curve of the shell, and the pearl was chosen last, set as the eye to give the turtle his gaze toward open water.
care: Dust gently with a dry, soft cloth and keep out of prolonged direct sun. Display indoors, away from moisture; avoid liquid cleaners on the glass and shell surfaces.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.

name: Rosé Flamingos
shortDescription: A pair of pink flamingos rendered in blush sea glass, mingling against soft white in a shadow-box frame.
description: Two flamingos lean toward one another in this delicate shadow box, their bodies and curving necks built from genuine pink and rose-blush sea glass, each frosted shard chosen for the way it catches the light like the bird's own feathers. Hand-inked legs, beaks and gentle eyes give the pair their poise, while the airy white interior of the deep shadow-box frame lets them float as if wading through morning mist. A quietly romantic piece — coastal whimsy made with restraint.
story: Flamingos have long been the Gulf Coast's unofficial emblem of warmth and easy living. Mary builds each bird from softly tumbled pink sea glass gathered along Englewood's shoreline, hand-inking every leg and beak so no two pairs ever stand quite the same way. Set deep within a clean white shadow box, this duo feels less like a souvenir and more like a small portrait of companionship by the water.
care: Dust gently with a dry, soft cloth and keep out of prolonged direct sun. Display indoors, away from moisture; avoid liquid cleaners on the glass and shell surfaces.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.

name: Aqua Seahorse
shortDescription: A graceful seahorse drifts in aqua sea glass above a cobalt seabed, finished with a single pearl and a glint of golden crystal.
description: A solitary seahorse rises through still water, its curved body composed entirely of soft aqua and seafoam crushed glass that catches the light like sunlit shallows. A single freshwater pearl marks the eye, while a cluster of golden pyrite crystals adds an unexpected shimmer along its cheek — a quiet luxury detail. Below, a scattering of cobalt, sky-blue and clear crushed glass forms a shifting seabed, grounding the piece in genuine coastal texture. Set within a clean white shadow-box frame, the composition floats with gallery-like calm, every fragment placed by hand.
story: Seahorses move slowly and deliberately through the Gulf grasses off Englewood, anchoring themselves with a curl of the tail while the tide passes around them. Mary Lee built this one fragment by fragment, layering aqua sea glass to echo the way light moves through warm shallow water. The golden crystal and lone pearl were added last — small graces, the kind of detail you notice only when you lean in close.
care: Dust gently with a dry, soft cloth and keep out of prolonged direct sun. Display indoors, away from moisture; avoid liquid cleaners on the glass and shell surfaces.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.

name: Cocktail Hour
shortDescription: Three sea-glass martinis — frosted gray, deep blue, and soft sea-green — raised on slender hand-painted stems in a clean white shadow box.
description: A spirited little still life made entirely from the sea. Three triangular pieces of genuine sea glass become martini bowls — one frosted dove gray, one deep cobalt blue, one soft sea-green — each balanced on a slender hand-painted black stem and footed base. Tumbled green glass and painted flourishes stand in for olives and a twist of garnish, the kind of details that reward a second look. Set against a bright white ground inside a crisp white shadow-box frame, the piece feels light, witty, and effortlessly coastal. A natural fit for a bar cart, butler's pantry, or a sunlit kitchen wall where good company gathers.
story: Every shard here began as something broken, carried and softened by the Gulf before Mary Lee gathered it along the Englewood shore. She arranges each frosted fragment by hand, then paints the stems and garnishes one delicate stroke at a time. No two martinis ever line up the same way — a one-of-a-kind toast to the water that made them.
care: Dust gently with a dry, soft cloth and keep out of prolonged direct sun. Display indoors, away from moisture; avoid liquid cleaners on the glass and shell surfaces.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.

name: Sunday Swimsuit Line
shortDescription: A row of little sea-glass swimsuits hung to dry on a hand-painted clothesline, in shades of turquoise and deep ocean blue.
description: A clothesline of tiny swimsuits, drying in the afternoon sun. This handmade shadow box arranges genuine sea glass into four playful bikinis and one-pieces, each strung from a slim hand-painted hanger along a single drawn line. The glass moves from bright turquoise to cobalt and deep navy, every piece tumbled smooth by the Gulf, every silhouette a little different. Set against a soft white ground inside a clean white shadow-box frame, it carries the easy, salt-rinsed charm of a beach cottage laundry day.
story: There is a particular kind of happiness in a swimsuit pinned to the line after a long swim, still cool and dripping, promising tomorrow will be just as warm. Mary gathered these blues and greens from the shoreline near Englewood and saw bathing suits in their soft, worn shapes. Each little suit is hung from a hanger she painted by hand, the line drawn in a single steady stroke, so the whole scene reads like a snapshot of summer drying in the breeze.
care: Dust gently with a dry, soft cloth and keep out of prolonged direct sun. Display indoors, away from moisture; avoid liquid cleaners on the glass and shell surfaces.

name: Gulf Coast Palm
shortDescription: A breeze-bent palm rendered in seafoam-green sea glass and a copper-flecked trunk, set against weathered grey wood in a deep shadow box.
description: A single palm leans into the Gulf wind, its fronds drawn from softly tumbled green sea glass that catches the light like fronds catch the afternoon sun. The trunk is built up in warm copper and amber crushed glass, each fragment hand-placed to suggest the texture of weathered bark. The whole scene floats over a washed weathered grey wood background within a deep, weathered wood shadow box, giving the piece a quiet sense of depth and dimension. Individually handmade from genuine sea glass and natural coastal materials, no two are ever exactly alike.
story: Inspired by the lone palms that line the Gulf shoreline near Englewood, Florida, this piece captures the way a single tree bends and sways against an endless coastal sky. Each frond and fragment of trunk is hand-laid by Mary Lee, one shard of sea glass at a time.
care: Dust gently with a dry, soft cloth and keep out of prolonged direct sun. Display indoors, away from moisture; avoid liquid cleaners on the glass and shell surfaces.

name: Little Tides
shortDescription: A tiny sea-glass clothesline of baby garments strung with hearts, framed in a crisp white shadow box.
description: Three little garments sway from a hand-drawn line in this tender shadow-box scene: a polka-dotted set, a soft dress, and a small onesie, each shaped from genuine sea glass in shades of seafoam and deep teal. The line is strung with hand-painted hearts that double as miniature clothespins, lending the piece a quiet, storybook charm. Set against a clean white ground in a deep shadow-box frame, it reads as both nursery art and coastal keepsake — the kind of piece that marks a beginning. Every fragment of glass was gathered and tumbled by the sea, then arranged by hand, so no two clotheslines are ever quite alike.
story: Mary beachcombs the Gulf shoreline near Englewood for frosted glass softened by years in the surf, choosing the gentlest seafoam and teal pieces for nursery work. Each garment is selected for its natural silhouette, then set by hand along a painted line of tiny hearts — a small celebration of new life, made entirely from what the sea returned.
care: Dust gently with a dry, soft cloth and keep out of prolonged direct sun. Display indoors, away from moisture; avoid liquid cleaners on the glass and shell surfaces.

name: Salt Air Laundry Day
shortDescription: A tiny clothesline of frosted aqua sea glass strung across a crisp white shadow box, each little "garment" hand-painted with dots and stripes.
description: A whisper of a seaside summer, captured in glass. Across the bright interior of a white shadow box, a slender wire is strung like a backyard clothesline, and from it hang three pieces of genuine frosted sea glass shaped like freshly laundered clothes — a pair of dotted little onesies, a softly striped shirt, and a polka-dotted piece swaying at the end. Each garment is hand-painted in pale aqua and seafoam, the brushwork delicate and unhurried, the glass still carrying the soft tumbled edges only the tide can give. It is quiet, tender, and quietly joyful: the kind of piece that makes a nursery, a laundry nook, or a coastal cottage feel cared for. Individually handmade in Englewood, Florida, and entirely one of a kind.
story: Mary gathered each of these little glass garments from Gulf Coast shorelines, then strung them on a fine wire and painted their dots and stripes by hand — a love letter to slow mornings, salt air, and laundry drying in the breeze.
care: Dust gently with a dry, soft cloth and keep out of prolonged direct sun. Display indoors, away from moisture; avoid liquid cleaners on the glass and shell surfaces.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.

seoDescription: A handmade sea glass shadow box of a coastal clothesline, with hand-painted aqua garments strung on a wire. One-of-a-kind coastal art from Englewood, Florida.

altText: White shadow box with three pieces of frosted aqua sea glass shaped like clothes hanging on a thin wire clothesline, hand-painted with dots and stripes.

name: Tide Line Garland
shortDescription: Four frosted aqua sea-glass shards strung like quiet bunting in a weathered whitewashed shadow box.
description: A spare, contemplative study in coastal calm. Four shards of genuine aqua sea glass — each softened to a sea-frosted glow by years in the Gulf — hang from a single fine line, swaying in stillness like a strand of beachcomber's bunting. Their gently graduated shapes catch the light differently across the day, shifting from pale celadon to a deeper tidewater blue. Set against a soft white ground and framed in heavily distressed whitewashed wood, the piece holds the airy, unhurried quality of a morning shoreline. Individually handmade and entirely one-of-a-kind.
story: Mary gathers her sea glass by hand along the quiet stretches of Englewood's Gulf shoreline, where the tide returns each piece smooth and frosted. For this garland she chose four shards in the same family of aqua, hung from a single line to echo the way found treasures are strung up to dry on a porch rail. The result is deliberately minimal — a celebration of negative space and the soft, watery color the sea leaves behind.
care: Dust gently with a dry, soft cloth and keep out of prolonged direct sun. Display indoors, away from moisture; avoid liquid cleaners on the glass and shell surfaces.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.
seoTitle: Tide Line Garland | Aqua Sea Glass Shadow Box
seoDescription: A minimalist coastal shadow box with four frosted aqua sea-glass shards strung in a delicate garland, framed in distressed whitewashed wood. Handmade in Englewood, Florida.
altText: Landscape whitewashed wood shadow box holding four frosted aqua sea-glass shards strung along a fine line against a white background.

name: Rose Quartz Tideline
shortDescription: Five frosted rose-pink sea glass shards suspended on a fine wire line inside a clean white shadow box.
description: A quiet study in soft rose and blush, this handmade shadow box gathers five pieces of genuine frosted sea glass and strings them gently along a slender dark wire that arcs across the frame like a tideline at dusk. Each shard carries its own translucent blush — some clear and glassy at the center, others clouded to a powdery pink by years in the surf — and no two are alike. Set against a bright white recessed ground and sealed behind glass, the composition feels airy and unhurried, a single breath of color caught and held. A serene, minimalist piece for a bedroom, bath, or any corner that wants a whisper of the sea rather than a shout.
story: Mary collects her glass by hand along the Gulf beaches near Englewood, and the rarest finds are the soft pinks — fragments tumbled smooth over years of tides. Rather than crowd them, she let just five breathe across a single wire, suspended like petals on a line, so each frosted shard could be seen for the small treasure it is.
care: Dust gently with a dry, soft cloth and keep out of prolonged direct sun. Display indoors, away from moisture; avoid liquid cleaners on the glass and shell surfaces.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.
seoTitle: Rose Quartz Tideline | Handmade Pink Sea Glass Shadow Box
seoDescription: A handmade coastal shadow box featuring five frosted rose-pink sea glass shards strung on a delicate wire inside a white frame. One-of-a-kind Gulf Coast art by Mary Lee.

name: Seas The Day
shortDescription: A sunlit "Seas the Day" shadow box layered with real shells, a finger starfish and barnacle, framed by painted teal coastal accents.
description: A serene reminder to live with the rhythm of the tide. Against a backdrop of sunlit, rippling water, the words "Seas the Day" rise in clean coastal lettering, surrounded by an arrangement of genuine seashells, a delicate white finger starfish and a sculptural barnacle cluster. The crisp white shadow box is anchored at each corner by hand-painted teal wood accents — a pair of starfish, a fish and an anchor — that frame the scene like a calm horizon. Each piece is placed by hand, making no two boxes exactly alike.
story: Created along Florida's Gulf Coast in Englewood, this shadow box gathers shells and starfish chosen one at a time, then arranged by hand around a phrase made for slow mornings and salt air. The painted wood accents are finished in a soft sea-foam teal that nods to the shallows just past the dune line. Like every Sea Attitudes piece, it is built to be lived with — a small, steady invitation to take the day as the sea takes the shore: gently, and all the way in.
care: Dust gently with a dry, soft cloth and keep out of prolonged direct sun. Display indoors, away from moisture; avoid liquid cleaners on the glass and shell surfaces.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.
seoTitle: Seas The Day Coastal Shadow Box | Handmade Shell Wall Art
seoDescription: Handmade "Seas the Day" coastal shadow box with real shells, finger starfish, barnacle and painted teal accents. One-of-a-kind beach wall art from Englewood, Florida.
altText: White shadow box reading "Seas the Day" over a sunlit water backdrop, framed by real shells, a white starfish, barnacle and painted teal starfish, anchor and fish accents.

name: Fly High, Free Bird
shortDescription: A flock of seven sea-glass songbirds gathered on a weathered wood branch beneath the hand-lettered words "Fly High, Free Bird."
description: Seven small birds, each shaped from a single piece of genuine tumbled sea glass, perch wing-to-wing along a length of weathered Gulf Coast wood. Their colors move down the line like a quiet melody — moss green, soft slate blue, sunlit yellow, and a single frosted-white fledgling at the center — each fitted with delicately inked legs and beaks. Above them, the words "Fly High, Free Bird" are hand-lettered in an unhurried script. Set within a clean white shadow-box frame with a deep recessed gap, the piece holds light and air around every bird, giving the little flock room to breathe.
story: Every bird here began as a wave-worn shard collected along the beaches of Englewood, Florida, its edges softened by years in the Gulf. Mary Lee sorts each fragment by color and curve, then arranges the flock by hand so no two birds — and no two pieces — are ever quite alike. The branch is weathered wood, chosen for the gentle arc it lends the composition. A tender keepsake for new beginnings, graduations, or remembering someone who taught you to spread your wings.
care: Dust gently with a dry, soft cloth and keep out of prolonged direct sun. Display indoors, away from moisture; avoid liquid cleaners on the glass and shell surfaces.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.

name: Always Stay Salty
shortDescription: A trio of sea-glass margaritas, each garnished with a lime wedge and a salted rim, beneath the hand-lettered words "Always Stay Salty."
description: Three frosted green margaritas line up across a crisp white ground, each one shaped from a genuine fragment of sea glass softened by years in the Gulf. A slim hand-lettered lime perches on every rim, while delicate dots of white trace the salted edge and a whisper of crushed glass glints at the base of each stem. Above them, "Always Stay Salty" is lettered by hand in a relaxed, easygoing script. Framed in a weathered gray shadow box, it is a small, witty piece made for a bar cart, a kitchen shelf, or the powder room of anyone who lives by the water and the cocktail hour alike.
story: Mary gathers her glass by hand along the Englewood shoreline, choosing only the pieces the tide has frosted smooth. Each margarita here began as a single sea-worn shard, set and lettered one at a time so that no two glasses tilt quite the same way. It is a reminder, she says, to keep a little salt in your spirit and a little sweetness in your glass.
care: Dust gently with a dry, soft cloth and keep out of prolonged direct sun. Display indoors, away from moisture; avoid liquid cleaners on the glass and shell surfaces.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.
seoTitle: Always Stay Salty Sea Glass Margarita Shadow Box | Sea Attitudes by Mary Lee
seoDescription: Handmade sea-glass margarita shadow box lettered "Always Stay Salty." Three frosted green cocktails with lime and salted rims in a weathered gray-wood frame. One-of-a-kind coastal art from Englewood, Florida.
altText: Sea-glass shadow box with three frosted green margarita glasses, lime wedges and salted rims, lettered "Always Stay Salty" in a weathered gray-wood frame.

name: Starfish Tide Pine
shortDescription: A luminous sea-glass Christmas tree in deep ocean blues, crowned with a natural knobby starfish and scattered with pearl accents.
description: Hand-built one shard at a time, this conical Christmas tree is mosaicked entirely in genuine sea glass that moves from sapphire and cerulean through soft aqua and frosted ice. A natural knobby starfish rests at the crown like a coastal star, while a handful of faux pearls drift across the branches like sea spray caught in candlelight. Lit softly from within, the glass glows with the same translucent depth as shallow Gulf water at dusk. Each tree is individually composed, so no two share the same arrangement of color or light.
story: Mary Lee builds her sea-glass trees the way the tide builds a shoreline, piece by patient piece. Working from her studio on Florida's Gulf Coast near Englewood, she sorts genuine sea glass by tone until the blues flow naturally from deepest ocean to palest foam, then sets each shard by hand around a glass cone. The knobby starfish at the top is chosen and placed last, a small nod to the night sky over the water. When lit, the tree carries the quiet of a coastal Christmas indoors.
care: Wipe gently with a dry, soft cloth and use only a low-heat LED light. Store wrapped in soft tissue between seasons, away from heavy objects.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.
seoTitle: Sea Glass Christmas Tree with Starfish Topper | Coastal Blue Lit Decor
seoDescription: Handmade lit sea-glass Christmas tree in ocean blues with a natural starfish topper and pearl accents. One-of-a-kind coastal holiday decor from Sea Attitudes by Mary Lee.
altText: Lit sea-glass Christmas tree in deep to pale ocean blues topped with a natural white knobby starfish and pearl accents

name: Starfish Harbor Glow
shortDescription: A glowing cone tree mosaicked in ocean-blue and sea-foam glass, crowned with a natural starfish and lit softly from within.
description: A coastal reimagining of the holiday tree, this freestanding cone is hand-set with genuine sea glass in tumbled shades of harbor blue, aqua and pale gold. Each fragment is placed by hand across the form, the seams left softly irregular so the light catching them feels like sun on shallow water. A warm interior glow rises through the glass, turning every piece luminous and amber-edged at the base. A single natural starfish rests at the crown in place of a star. Lit or unlit, it brings the quiet of the Gulf shoreline to a mantel, entry table or windowsill.
story: Made along the Gulf Coast in Englewood, Florida, this tree began as a plain glass cone and was patiently tiled with sea glass gathered and tumbled for its color. Mary Lee fits each shard like a small tide pool, then sets a beachcombed starfish at the top so the season arrives the way it does here — softly, by the water, never with snow.
care: Wipe gently with a dry, soft cloth and use only a low-heat LED light. Store wrapped in soft tissue between seasons, away from heavy objects.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.

altText: Illuminated cone Christmas tree covered in blue, aqua and gold sea glass with a natural white starfish on top, glowing on a dark wood table.

name: Cobalt Tide Pine
shortDescription: A glowing cone of cobalt and sea-foam sea glass, crowned with a natural starfish and lit softly from within.
description: Hand-built one shard at a time, this luminous tabletop tree layers genuine sea glass in deep cobalt, soft sky blue and pale sea-foam green over a tapering cone. Warm LED lights tucked inside catch in the translucent glass, so the whole piece seems to glow from the tide line outward. A natural sugar starfish sits at the crown in place of a star, and a dusting of crushed-glass "snow" rings the turned wood base. A coastal heirloom for the holidays, equally at home on a mantel in December or a console table all year long.
story: Mary builds each tree by hand on Florida's Gulf Coast, fitting and setting every piece of glass individually so no two ever align the same way. The cobalt and sea-foam tones echo the water just off Englewood at dusk, and the starfish topper is chosen by hand for its symmetry. Lit from within, it carries the calm of the shore into the season.
care: Wipe gently with a dry, soft cloth and use only a low-heat LED light. Store wrapped in soft tissue between seasons, away from heavy objects.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.

altText: Lit sea-glass Christmas tree in cobalt blue and sea-foam green glass with a natural starfish topper, glowing on a turned wood base atop a dark wood table.

name: Moonlit Tide Pine
materials: frosted glass base with LED light
shortDescription: A lit cone of moonlit blue and lavender sea glass that glows softly from within.
description: Tumbled sea glass in moonlit blues and soft lavender is laid piece by piece up a tapered cone, then lit gently from inside so the whole tree glows like a tide pool at dusk. A dusting of crushed glass frosts the base. Set it on a mantel or entry table and it turns an evening coastal.
story: Mary made the first of these the winter she missed snow least of all — trading frost for the colors the Gulf leaves behind at twilight. Each piece of glass catches the internal light differently, so no two trees ever glow quite the same.
care: Wipe gently with a dry, soft cloth and use only a low-heat LED light. Store wrapped in soft tissue between seasons, away from heavy objects.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.

name: Salty Sayings Shell Dishes
shortDescription: A genuine scallop shell hand-painted in a luminous ombre wash and finished with a coastal saying — a little catch-all dish for rings, earrings, and everyday treasures.
description: Each of these dishes begins as a real scallop shell, gathered from the Gulf shore and hand-painted in a soft ombre that deepens from pale, sun-bleached edges to a saturated heart of turquoise, amethyst, magenta, or burnished gold. A coastal saying is lettered across the basin in crisp white, then the whole shell is sealed under a clear glaze so it can hold rings, earrings, trinkets, or a single sprig of sea lavender. Choose from "Let Your Dreams Set Sail," "The Beach Is Where I Belong," "Salty Hair Don't Care," "This Little Mermaid Is Salty," and "Eat Beach Sleep Repeat." Because each shell is found and finished by hand, no two share the exact same curve, color drift, or natural ridge.
story: Mary keeps a basket of scallop shells by her studio window, turning each one over until the ridges suggest a color. The ombre is brushed wet so the pigment bleeds the way tide pulls across wet sand, and only then does the saying go on — small reminders to stay salty, dream wide, and let the beach be where you belong.
care: Wipe gently with a soft, dry cloth. Natural shells and sea glass; keep out of prolonged direct sun and standing water.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.
seoTitle: Painted Scallop Shell Trinket Dishes with Coastal Sayings | Sea Attitudes by Mary Lee
seoDescription: Handmade scallop shell ring dishes painted in luminous ombre with coastal sayings like "Salty Hair Don't Care" and "Eat Beach Sleep Repeat." Each one a one-of-a-kind Gulf Coast keepsake.
altText: Five hand-painted scallop shell trinket dishes in turquoise, gold, purple, magenta, and teal ombre, each lettered with a coastal saying.
productType: Painted sea-glass scallop shell trinket dish set

name: Gilded Tide Stars
materials: hand-applied acrylic in coastal blue tones
shortDescription: A set of genuine starfish hand-painted in soft coastal blues and touched with gold leaf, each one entirely one-of-a-kind.
description: Four genuine starfish, each gathered and hand-finished in our Englewood studio, painted in a gradient of soft Gulf blues from misty seafoam to deep harbor teal. Their natural ridged surfaces are highlighted with hand-applied gold leaf, catching the light along the raised nodules and tapered arms. No two stars are alike — the painting follows each star's own texture, so every piece carries its own pattern of gilded accents. Sealed to preserve their natural form, they are made to be styled together as a wall cluster, tucked into a bookshelf, or given singly as a small luxury keepsake.
story: Mary gathers each starfish by hand and lets its natural architecture guide the brush, layering coastal blue over the textured surface before pressing whispers of gold leaf into the ridges. The gilding is never uniform; it settles where the star wants it, so the four together read like a small tide pool caught at golden hour. They are the kind of quiet, collectible piece meant to be lived with and passed along.
care: Wipe gently with a soft, dry cloth. Natural shells and sea glass; keep out of prolonged direct sun and standing water.
shipping: Each piece is hand-wrapped and shipped from Englewood, Florida within 3–5 business days, fully insured. Ships nationwide; complimentary Gulf Coast local delivery available on request.

altText: Four genuine starfish hand-painted in soft coastal blue with gold leaf accents on their ridged arms.
